1.title: A book from an Egyptian scroll
1.text: The Book of Abraham is part of the Pearl of Great Price. Latter-day Saints hold it as Abraham's own record, written in the first person — notice how chapter 1 opens with 'I, Abraham.'

1.question: In Abraham 1:1, what did Abraham find he needed to obtain?
In Abraham 1:5, why did Abraham's fathers refuse to hearken to his voice?
options: They had turned to worshiping the gods of the heathen

1.template: they would not bow down to worship gods of {0} or of stone

1.question: According to Abraham 1:15-16, who delivered Abraham as the priest lifted his hands to slay him?
options: The Lord, who declared his name is Jehovah

1.question: In Abraham 1:19, the Lord says that as it was with whom, so shall it be with Abraham?
In Abraham 1:21-24, from whose loins was the king of Egypt descended?
According to Abraham 1:28-30, what did the Lord preserve in Abraham's own hands?
options: The records of the fathers concerning the right of Priesthood

2.question: In Abraham 2:3-4, to what land did the Lord command Abraham to go?
options: A land the Lord would show him

2.template: I will {0} them that bless thee, and curse them that curse thee

2.title: Sixty-two years old
2.text: Abraham 2:14 gives a rare, specific detail: Abraham was 'sixty and two years old' when he departed out of Haran — a reminder that this is written as a personal, remembered record.

2.question: In Abraham 2:15-16, whom did Abraham take with him as he journeyed toward Canaan?
In Abraham 2:22-23, what did the Lord warn about Sarai as they neared Egypt?
options: She would be made a queen
The Egyptians would see she was fair and kill Abraham

3.question: In Abraham 3:2-3, what is the name of the great star nearest to the throne of God?

3.title: A day is a thousand years
3.text: Abraham 3:4 says one revolution of Kolob is 'a day unto the Lord,' being 'one thousand years' by earthly reckoning — an idea echoed in 2 Peter 3:8.

3.question: In Abraham 3:9, one comes near the throne of God as one comes near what?
According to Abraham 3:18, what is said of the two spirits — that they are 'gnolaum'?
options: They are eternal, having no beginning and no end
They are equal in all things

3.question: In Abraham 3:22-23, the Lord tells Abraham he was among the noble and great ones and was chosen when?
In Abraham 3:27, who answers 'Here am I, send me' and is chosen — the Lord says 'I will send the first'?
options: The one like unto the Son of Man

4.title: The Gods organized
4.text: Unlike Genesis, Abraham 4 says the Gods 'organized and formed' the heavens and the earth — organizing existing materials rather than creating from nothing.

4.question: In Abraham 4:5, what did the Gods call the light and the darkness?

4.template: And the Gods called the expanse, {0}

4.question: According to Abraham 4:14, what did the Gods organize the lights in the expanse to be for?
In Abraham 4:16, which of the two great lights was set to rule the day?
In Abraham 4:24-25, on which 'time' did the Gods prepare the earth to bring forth cattle and beasts?
According to Abraham 4:27, the Gods formed man in whose image?
options: In the image of the earth
In the image of the Gods, male and female
In the image of the beasts

5.question: In Abraham 5:2-3, on which 'time' did the Gods rest from all their work?

5.title: Time reckoned by Kolob
5.text: Abraham 5:13 pauses to note that this was 'after the Lord's time, which was after the time of Kolob' — tying the creation account back to the astronomy of chapter 3.

5.question: In Abraham 5:11-13, what did the Gods command about the tree of knowledge of good and evil?
options: That man should not eat of it

5.question: In Abraham 5:14-17, why did the Gods form a help meet for the man?
options: Because it was not good that the man should be alone
